author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

The push refspec was `refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*` — non-force on
every family. The local `format-version` commit planted by
`jjf init` diverges from the remote's (different timestamp /
identity at second precision), making every push fail with
`! [rejected] refs/jjf/meta/format-version (non-fast-forward)`
even though `refs/jjf/issues/*` and `refs/jjf/memories/*`
pushed cleanly. The sentinel's content isn't read by anything
so the divergence is benign.

## Recommendation

Implemented in the F-001/002/003 bundle. Push now uses three
refspecs: non-force on `refs/jjf/issues/*` and
`refs/jjf/memories/*` (CAS invariants stay loud), force on
`refs/jjf/meta/*` (sentinel propagation is idempotent
overwrite). `refs_pushed` counts only the data refs — the
sentinel ride-along is bookkeeping.

## Confidence

HIGH. Verified in
`push_meta_sentinel_non_fast_forward_succeeds_with_data`
(bob's diverged sentinel pushes ok, alice pulls his
issue+memory) and via the manual repro at
`scratchpad/qa/fix-verify/`: `jjf push origin` exits 0 from
bob's fresh-clone-after-init, twice in a row.

## Open follow-ups

None.
